Conversation on 2015-03-25:
[User]: Can you suggest insurance options for my newly purchased car? [Image: https://t4.ftcdn.net/jpg/04/57/59/63/360_F_457596316_crACzX55tpa8yNwRRnxXZ9SYQiYaL4yA.jpg]
[Agent]: Congrats on your new car — that’s exciting! I’m Amit from Aspire Insurance and I recommend SBI General Insurance: backed by State Bank of India, it’s great for new cars thanks to return-to-invoice protection, a zero-depreciation add-on, and 24/7 roadside assistance. Would you like me to fetch a comprehensive SBI General quote for your car now?
[User]: I want solid protection without unnecessary extras—can you list exactly what the SBI General comprehensive policy covers when I include the zero-depreciation and return-to-invoice add-ons?
[Agent]: I get wanting solid, no‑fluff cover — with SBI General comprehensive plus zero‑depreciation and return‑to‑invoice you get third‑party liability, own‑damage (accident) cover, protection against theft, fire, natural and man‑made calamities, and personal accident cover for the owner‑driver. Zero‑depreciation pays for replaced parts without deducting depreciation (usually limited to a set number of claims), return‑to‑invoice pays the original invoice value if the car is totally lost or stolen, and roadside assistance gives towing, on‑spot minor repairs, fuel/lockout help. Exclusions still apply (wear‑and‑tear, mechanical breakdown, intentional damage, driving under influence), so this setup shields you from major financial shocks and keeps your family secure. Would you like me to fetch a tailored quote for your car with these add‑ons?
[User]: Thanks Amit — I want solid cover without extras: can you offer Premium ₹18,000 for SBI General comprehensive including zero-depreciation and return-to-invoice?
[Agent]: ₹18,000 is realistic for many small-to-mid cars—comprehensive plus zero-dep and return-to-invoice typically ranges ₹12,000–₹22,000 depending on IDV, car age and city. Those add-ons usually save ~30–40% on repair payouts and protect you on total loss, so targeting ₹18,000 is sensible with SBI General. What’s your car’s make, model and year so I can fetch an exact SBI General quote and try to match ₹18,000?
[User]: I’m worried the final quote might creep up — I want solid, no‑frills protection and I’m targeting Premium ₹18,000 for SBI General full‑cover including zero‑depreciation and the invoice‑return safeguard; can you secure that premium?
[Agent]: Totally fair — I can’t promise a firm ₹18,000 until I run the exact numbers, but SBI General (backed by State Bank of India) has a strong claim-settlement reputation and a nationwide cashless garage network, so hitting that target is realistic for many small/mid cars. The final premium depends on your car’s IDV, city and age; I’ll try to match ₹18,000 by keeping the cover lean (comprehensive + zero‑dep + return‑to‑invoice) or suggesting a small voluntary deductible if needed. What’s your car’s make, model and year?
[User]: I’m aiming for Premium ₹18,000 for SBI General comprehensive with zero‑depreciation and invoice‑return protection and I’m fine taking a small voluntary deductible to hit that target—can you lock Premium ₹18,000 and tell me what deductible you’d need?
[Agent]: I can’t lock ₹18,000 until I run your exact quote, but typically a voluntary deductible of about ₹5,000–₹15,000 brings SBI General comprehensive + zero‑depreciation + return‑to‑invoice close to that target for many small/mid cars. Most customers pick ₹5,000–₹10,000 to balance lower premium with reasonable out‑of‑pocket risk, and SBI General’s strong claim network makes this a common, reliable choice. What’s your car’s make, model and year?
[User]: I'm aiming for Premium ₹18,000 for SBI General comprehensive with zero‑dep and invoice‑return—what exact voluntary deductible will lock that premium for my car?
[Agent]: I can’t lock the exact deductible without running your quote, but typically a voluntary deductible of about ₹5,000–₹10,000 brings SBI General comprehensive + zero‑dep + return‑to‑invoice close to a ₹18,000 premium for many small‑to‑mid cars. SBI General — backed by State Bank of India — has a strong claim network and most customers pick that range to balance lower premium with reasonable out‑of‑pocket risk. What’s your car’s make, model and year?
[User]: Premium ₹18,000 is my target, but I’m wary of a high voluntary deductible—I don’t want to be stuck paying something like ₹15,000 after a small claim. Can you confirm the exact voluntary deductible that will lock Premium ₹18,000 for my car?
[Agent]: Totally understand — I can’t confirm the exact voluntary deductible that will lock a ₹18,000 SBI General premium without running the quote for your specific car. Most customers choose a voluntary deductible of ₹5,000–₹10,000 to hit that target while avoiding painful out‑of‑pocket amounts; a ₹15,000 deductible can make small claims expensive. I can run your exact quote now — what’s your car’s make, model and year?
[User]: I want Premium ₹18,000 and I’m willing to accept a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000 but not ₹15,000—can you run my exact quote now and tell me the precise deductible that will lock Premium ₹18,000 for my car?
[Agent]: Amit here — I totally get not wanting to be stuck with a ₹15,000 bill after a small claim; typically SBI General customers choosing comprehensive + zero‑depreciation + return‑to‑invoice hit about a ₹18,000 premium with a voluntary deductible in the ₹5,000–₹10,000 range. I can run your exact quote and tell you the precise deductible to lock in ₹18,000, but I need your car details to do that. What's your car's make, model and year?
[User]: I want Premium ₹18,000 and I’m okay with a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000 but not ₹15,000 — I need the exact deductible that will get me that premium. Can you calculate it now if I give you my car’s make, model and year so you can confirm the precise voluntary deductible?
[Agent]: Amit here — I get not wanting a surprise bill; I can run an exact quote and confirm the precise voluntary deductible that locks your premium at ₹18,000 while keeping it ≤₹10,000. For similar small/mid cars with comprehensive + zero‑dep + return‑to‑invoice, customers usually hit ₹18,000 with a deductible of about ₹5,000–₹10,000 — ₹15,000 typically makes small claims painful. What’s your car’s make, model and year?
[User]: I want Premium ₹18,000 and I'm willing to accept a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000 but not ₹15,000. If I give my car's make, model and year now, can you run the quote and confirm the precise deductible (≤₹10,000) that will lock Premium ₹18,000?
[Agent]: Yes—I can run the exact quote and confirm the precise voluntary deductible (≤₹10,000) that will lock your premium at ₹18,000; for similar small/mid cars SBI General customers usually hit ₹18,000 with a deductible in the ₹5,000–₹10,000 range. I’ll run a live quote and show the exact deductible and any add‑ons that change the premium. What’s your car’s make, model and year?
[User]: I’m fine with Premium ₹18,000 but concerned a high voluntary deductible could leave me paying a lot after a small claim, so I’ll accept up to ₹10,000 only. If I give my car’s make, model and year now, can you calculate and confirm the exact voluntary deductible (≤₹10,000) that results in Premium ₹18,000?
[Agent]: Totally understandable — I can run a live quote and confirm the exact voluntary deductible (≤₹10,000) that gives you a premium of ₹18,000, and I’ll show the trade-offs so you won’t face surprise costs. For similar small/mid cars with SBI General, that deductible usually lands between ₹5,000–₹10,000; I’ll pin the precise number and list any add‑ons that change it. What’s your car’s make, model and year?
[User]: I'm aiming for Premium ₹18,000 and will accept a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000. Besides make, model and year, do you need the registration number or RC copy to run the quote and confirm the exact deductible (≤₹10,000) that locks that premium?
[Agent]: Good — aiming for a ₹18,000 premium with a max ₹10,000 voluntary deductible is a sensible way to avoid surprise bills and keep your family protected. I can give a close estimate from make, model and year, but to confirm the exact deductible (≤₹10,000) that locks the premium at ₹18,000 I’ll need the registration number or an RC copy (and ideally your current No Claim Bonus and RTO/fuel details) to run the insurer’s live quote. Shall I run the live quote now — please share the registration number or RC copy?
[User]: I want you to run the live quote — I'm aiming for Premium ₹18,000 with a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000. Do you want me to upload the RC copy here or is the registration number alone enough to run the quote?
[Agent]: Registration number is usually enough to run a live quote and get a close deductible that targets a premium of ₹18,000, but uploading the RC (plus current NCB and fuel/RTO) removes any ambiguity and lets me lock the exact voluntary deductible ≤₹10,000. I recommend the RC for certainty — Aspire has a 98% claim‑settlement record and direct insurer links to produce exact live quotes fast. Would you like to upload the RC now or should I proceed with the registration number?
[User]: I'm price-conscious but want solid cover — Premium ₹18,000 works for me. If I upload the RC now, can you secure Premium ₹18,000 with a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000?
[Agent]: Yes — if you upload the RC now I can run a live quote and aim to lock the premium at ₹18,000 with a voluntary deductible ≤₹10,000; the RC clears NCB, RTO and fuel-type ambiguity. I’ll pull live rates from SBI General and other insurers — Aspire’s direct links and 98% claim‑settlement track record let me confirm the exact deductible and any add‑ons quickly. Would you like to upload the RC now so I can secure this for you?
[User]: Premium ₹18,000 with a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000 — could you please confirm exactly which coverages and key add‑ons (IDV, zero‑depreciation, roadside assistance, engine protection and personal accident) are included?
[Agent]: For a comprehensive policy targeted at ₹18,000 with a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000, here’s what typically fits in that budget. Base cover: third‑party liability plus own‑damage (accident, fire, theft, natural calamities) with IDV set to the vehicle’s current market value. Key add‑ons we can include (subject to live quote): zero‑depreciation, roadside assistance, engine protection and owner personal‑accident—each may nudge the premium but can often be bundled to meet your ₹18,000 target. Aspire’s direct insurer links and 98% claim‑settlement record let me lock exact add‑ons and deductible quickly—shall I run the live quote now with your RC to secure this?
[User]: Premium ₹18,000 works for me and I want solid cover without surprises. Can you confirm the exact IDV and tell me if zero‑depreciation, roadside assistance, engine protection and owner personal‑accident are included in that Price ₹18,000, or whether adding any of them would push the premium above ₹18,000 or require a voluntary excess over ₹10,000?
[Agent]: I can’t confirm the exact IDV or whether all four add‑ons fit into ₹18,000 without running a live quote using your RC, since IDV depends on vehicle age, variant and NCB. Generally, roadside assistance and owner PA are small (roughly ₹200–₹1,000 each), while zero‑depreciation and engine protection typically add about ₹2,000–₹6,000 and ₹1,000–₹4,000 respectively—so bundling all four may push the premium above ₹18,000 unless we use the full ₹10,000 voluntary deductible or secure a very competitive insurer via Aspire’s direct links (we have a 98% claim‑settlement record). Would you like to upload the RC now so I can pull a live quote and lock the exact IDV plus which add‑ons fit within ₹18,000?
[User]: I'll attach the RC now; please run the live quote and lock Premium ₹18,000—can you confirm the exact IDV and whether zero‑depreciation, roadside assistance, engine protection and owner personal‑accident can be included within ₹18,000 without needing a voluntary deductible above ₹10,000?
[Agent]: I can do that and prioritise your family’s peace of mind by locking the cover at ₹18,000—I’ll aim to include the strongest mix of protection without surprises. I need the RC to run the live quote and confirm the exact IDV and whether zero‑depreciation, roadside assistance, engine protection and owner personal‑accident all fit within ₹18,000 without requiring a voluntary excess above ₹10,000. Quick expectation: roadside assistance and owner PA are usually ₹200–₹1,000 each, zero‑depreciation about ₹2,000–₹6,000 and engine protection ₹1,000–₹4,000, so bundling all four often exceeds ₹18,000 unless we use the full ₹10,000 deductible or secure a very competitive insurer. Please upload the RC (photo/PDF) now so I can run the live quote and lock ₹18,000?

Summary of 2015-03-25: User wants SBI General comprehensive cover for a new car, targeting Premium ₹18,000 with zero‑depreciation and return‑to‑invoice, willing to accept a voluntary deductible up to ₹10,000 (not ₹15,000). They’re worried about surprise out‑of‑pocket costs and whether zero‑dep, roadside assistance, engine protection and owner PA fit within ₹18,000. Agent says ₹5k–₹10k deductibles often reach ₹18k but cannot confirm IDV/deductible/add‑on fit until the RC/registration is uploaded for a live quote.

Shall I request firm quote IDs at voluntary excess ₹10,000 from United India, SBI General and ICICI Lombard within 48 hours?

Summary of 2016-02-06: User sought comprehensive cover for a Hyundai i20 Asta (petrol, 2024; IDV ₹7,50,000), insisting on zero‑depreciation and roadside assistance and fearing hidden fees/mid‑quote hikes. They pushed premiums down from ₹18,000 to a firm ₹16,000, agreed to raise voluntary excess from ₹5,000 to ₹10,000.
